name: Pad Phed Pladuk (Hot & Spicy Catfish).
recipeInstructions:
1. Use the grated coconut to make 1-1/4 cups of coconut milk by adding 1/2 cup very hot water and working the mixture thoroughly, then squeezing out the coconut milk. Canned (unsweetened) coconut milk can be substituted.
2. Cut catfish fillets into medium cubes about 2-inch x 2-inch.
3. Discard seeds from dried chilli and soak in cold water for at least half an hour.
4. Separate about 1/2 cup "cream" from the coconut milk, heat it in a frying pan, and fry the fish pieces until just cooked. Set aside.
5. In a mortar, pound salt, peppercorns, cumin, coriander seeds, mace, cardamom, and nutmeg until well mixed into a fine pulp. Drain dried chilli (discard water) and add to the paste. Continue pounding until chilli pieces are mixed in. Add lemongrass, galanga, kaffir lime rind, cilantro roots, krachai, and fresh Thai chilli. Continue pounding until a fine well-mixed paste is formed. Premade Thai Red Curry Paste can be substituted.
6. Combine the curry paste with the remaining coconut milk and fry over high heat until boiling. Lower to medium heat and continue frying while stirring until oil forms on top. Add cooked fish pieces and coconut "cream" mixture. Lower to low heat and gently cook until thoroughly heated. Adjust flavor with fish sauce and a little sugar for a slight sweet taste. Add several kaffir lime leaves and cook longer.
7. Serve hot over plain boiled white rice, garnished with slices of fresh red chili peppers.
